---
title: "W. Jonas Reger"
image: "assets/images/profile.jpg"
title-block-banner: false
about:
  id: about-heading
  template: "solana"
  links:
    - icon: email
      text: Email
      href: mailto:wjonasreger@gmail.com
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/wjonasreger/
    - icon: github
      text: Github
      href: https://github.com/wjonasreger
---

::: {#about-heading}
My name is Jonas! I am a Graduate Student studying Data Science within the Statistics Department at the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign (UIUC). I taught Statistics and Data Science in various roles as an Instructor and Teaching Assistant in university courses as well as research and education programs for equity, diversity, and inclusion for students of diverse backgrounds.

I am a self-taught programmer of 10+ years with great interests in applying my computational skills to diverse fields related to Data Science. While my general interests extends broadly across Data Science, **my core interests are Natural Language Processing and Machine Learning**.
:::

## Skills

-   Machine Learning, Natural Language Processing, Data Management, Statistical Computing, Statistical Data Analysis, Programming, Mathematical Theory
-   Python: (Jupyter, NumPy, Pandas, Scikit-Learn, TensorFlow, PyTorch, NLTK, Seaborn, BeautifulSoup, discord.py)
-   R: (Rstudio, ggplot2, dplyr, caret, tidyverse, purr, shiny, plotly)
-   Other: (Jekyll, Quarto, Git/GitHub, GitHub Pages, C++, Java, SQL, Javascript, HTML, CSS)

## Experience

**Sandia National Laboratories --- R&D Graduate Intern \[May 2022---Aug 2022\]**

-   Examined social media data to ascertain evidence of social influence strategy
-   Scraped data from Reddit using Python and PushShift API wrappers
-   Utilized Huggingface Natural Language Processing models for sentiment, topic, intent, and utterance analysis
-   Developed Random Forest and Deep Neural Network models for predicting user engagement

## Teaching

**Details**

-   Facilitated student learning objectives in class and office hours covering data science topics in programming with Python/R, data management, probability, and statistics
-   Wrote and audited course materials for curriculum development and student assessment using Python/R and GitHub
-   Supported students from diverse backgrounds in acquiring data science skills and knowledge
-   Designed and maintained the STAT 100 course website using Jekyll and Github Pages, which serves 1000+ students each semester, providing useful course information and materials

**Instructor**

-   `STAT 100` Statistics \[[`sp22`](https://stat100website.web.illinois.edu/archive/S22/pages/staff.html)\]
-   DPI Discovering Data Science \[[`su21`](https://wjonasreger.github.io/DigitalScholars_DataScience/pages/about.html)\]

**Teaching Assistant**

-   `STAT 107` Data Science Discovery \[[`fa21`](https://discovery.cs.illinois.edu/stat107-fa21/staff/)\]
-   `STAT 385` Statistics Programming Methods \[[`fa22`](https://stat385.org/)\]
-   `STAT 430` Data Science Ethics \[`sp23`\]

**Course Assistant**

-   `STAT 100` Statistics \[[`fa21`](https://stat100website.web.illinois.edu/archive/F21/pages/staff.html) [`sp21`](https://stat100website.web.illinois.edu/archive/S21/pages/staff.html) [`fa20`](https://stat100website.web.illinois.edu/archive/F20/pages/staff.html) `su20` [`sp20`](https://stat100website.web.illinois.edu/archive/S20/pages/staff.html) `fa19` `sp19`\]
-   `STAT 400` Statistics & Probability I \[`sp20` `sp19`\]
-   `STAT 410` Statistics & Probability II \[`sp20`\]
-   `STAT 420` Methods of Applied Statistics \[`fa19` `su19`\]
-   `STAT 432` Basics of Statistical Learning \[[`fa20`](https://fall-2020.stat432.org/)\]
-   Blackwell Summer Scholars Program \[[`su21`](https://stat.illinois.edu/alumni/david-blackwell/blackwell-summer-scholars-program)\]

## Education

**University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign --- Champaign, IL**

-   M.S. Statistics --- May 2023
-   B.S. Statistics, Mathematics Minor --- May 2021

**Parkland College --- Champaign, IL**

-   A.S. Computer Science --- May 2018
